festivals.name: Carnaval de Barranquilla
festivals.description: Latin America's second largest carnival is a UNESCO masterpiece of four days of parades, cumbia, and marimonda masks.

festivals.name: Feria de Cali
festivals.description: The world capital of salsa erupts for six nights of concerts, parades, and dancing by three million visitors.

destinations.name: Bogotá
destinations.description: Colombia's vibrant highland capital has the world's best Gold Museum, La Candelaria's colonial quarter, and street art.

destinations.name: Cartagena
destinations.description: A perfectly preserved Spanish colonial walled city on the Caribbean with colourful balconies and a legendary party scene.

destinations.name: Medellín
destinations.description: The world's most transformed city has gone from notorious to a model for urban innovation with cable cars and botanic gardens.

destinations.name: Coffee Region (Salento)
destinations.description: Colombia's UNESCO coffee landscape of lush mountains, colorful haciendas, and Los Nevados national park.

destinations.name: Tayrona National Park
destinations.description: Lush rainforest meets pristine Caribbean beaches where ancient Tairona stone paths lead to jungle-framed coves.

destinations.name: Ciudad Perdida (Lost City)
destinations.description: A 5-day jungle trek leads to a pre-Columbian city of terraced circles older than Machu Picchu, still used by indigenous Kogi.

destinations.name: Cali
destinations.description: The world capital of salsa where the dance is a way of life — take classes in a local salsa school before hitting the clubs.

destinations.name: San Agustín
destinations.description: A UNESCO site in the upper Magdalena with hundreds of pre-Columbian stone statues and burial mounds in a green landscape.
